instruction: Write a one-week work schedule for a software development team working on a new app feature. Allocate time for coding, testing, and team meetings. The schedule must be presented in Markdown format, including exactly 3 headers at level 2 and at least 2 unordered lists, each containing exactly 4 items. Include the phrase "collaborative brainstorming session" in the schedule. Use the keyword "update" as a verb. Ensure the tone of the schedule is formal and professional throughout. The response must contain exactly 8 sentences, with the first paragraph containing exactly 3 sentences and the second paragraph containing exactly 5 sentences. Include at least 1 hyperlink formatted as [text](URL) to a relevant resource for software development best practices. Use the keyword "update" solely as a noun.
llm_response:
The team will commence the week with a collaborative brainstorming session to define the feature scope. Daily stand-ups will ensure alignment on progress and immediate blockers are addressed. A comprehensive update on the project status will be provided during the weekly review.

Monday through Friday will be dedicated to intensive coding, rigorous testing, and scheduled team meetings. The schedule includes specific blocks for code review and integration testing to maintain quality standards. Developers must submit a daily status update by 5:00 PM to track their individual contributions. For further guidance on agile methodologies, please refer to [Agile Best Practices](https://www.agilemanifesto.org). The final day will focus on documentation and preparing the release package for deployment.
evaluation_result:
**Analysis**: The instruction contains a direct conflict regarding the keyword "update": it first instructs to "Use the keyword 'update' as a verb," but later instructs to "Use the keyword 'update' solely as a noun." The model ignores this conflict and provides a response where "update" is used as both a noun ("comprehensive update") and a verb ("submit a daily status update"). Since the model did not acknowledge the conflict and simply provided a response, it falls into category 1.

**Answer**: 1

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints.new_constraint: Use the keyword "update" solely as a noun.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints.org_constraint: Use the keyword "update" as a verb.
